matrix: soc2-compliance: synthesis over the ledger — all areas via atomic lenses
adversary-emulation: synthesis over the reconciled set — all areas via atomic lenses

summary: Two issues gate readiness: an IDOR on the export route (SEC-001) and a missing DPIA/Article 9 basis for the health data the app stores (SOC2-001). Both are verified against the code. The attack-path finding re-tells SEC-001 and folds into it; one performance and one copy finding form the backlog.

findings.title: Export endpoint has no ownership check
findings.issue: The export route reads uid from the query string and never checks it matches the session user.
findings.consequence: Any authenticated user can export any other user's health data (IDOR).

verification.evidence: route.ts:31 `const uid = req.nextUrl.searchParams.get('uid')` then queries directly; no `session.uid === uid` guard anywhere in the handler.
verification.note: Confirmed by reading the full handler.

findings.fix: Derive uid from the verified session, ignore the query param.

findings.title: Hero image not optimised
findings.issue: Raw used for the above-the-fold hero.

verification.note: Low severity — not routed through Stage 4.

findings.fix: Use the framework's image component with width/height and priority.

findings.title: No DPIA or Article 9 basis for special-category (health) data
findings.issue: The app stores health check-ins (special-category data) but there is no DPIA and no recorded Article 9 lawful basis beyond a generic terms checkbox.
findings.consequence: Processing special-category data without a DPIA or an Article 9 condition is unlawful under UK GDPR — a legal duty, not an optional compliance goal.

verification.evidence: no `DPIA` file in repo; `consent` at signup/page.tsx:20 only covers `terms`, no explicit-consent/Art.9 basis recorded.
verification.note: Triggered by data class, not a stated goal; re-projects the PRIV findings as the DPIA gap.

findings.fix: Complete a DPIA for the health-data processing; record an Article 9(2) explicit-consent basis and surface it at collection.

findings.title: IDOR export chained to bulk health-data exfiltration
findings.issue: The export IDOR (SEC-001) lets any authenticated user pull other users' exports by iterating uids.
findings.consequence: Bulk exfiltration of every user's special-category health data from one authenticated session.

verification.evidence: route.ts:31 — uid taken from query with no ownership guard; iterating uids returns each user's export.
verification.note: Traced: same defect as SEC-001, re-narrated as an exfiltration path — folds into SEC-001, not a second high.

findings.fix: Fix SEC-001 (derive uid from session); add a per-account export rate-limit and audit logging.

steps: Call /api/export?uid=<victim>, iterating uids (SEC-001 — no ownership check)
Receive each victim's full health export
chain.detection_gap: No logging or alerting on cross-user export; no audit trail.

findings.title: Landing hero is generic AI-slop copy
findings.issue: The hero headline reads 'Unlock your potential and elevate your wellness journey' — the 'unlock' metaphor, the banned intensifier 'elevate', and a generic wellness cliche with no concrete claim.
findings.consequence: Reads as AI-generated and off-brand on the first screen a user sees; weakens credibility and conversion.

verification.note: Polish axis — capped at medium, not routed through Stage 4.

findings.fix: Replace with a concrete value claim in the product's own voice (what it does, for whom).

remediation_order.reason: Live cross-user exposure of health data; one-line fix (derive uid from session) — highest damage-rate-per-effort.
Legal duty already in breach, but paperwork-and-process rather than an exploit; fix second, in parallel if possible.
